Composition.Programmer 1: Fix and enhance main.py to ensure all Flask routes correctly render their respective templates, including adding error handling for missing data and improving data reading functions if needed. Specifically, fix the dashboard() function to handle the missing 'dashboard.html' template scenario gracefully and prepare the backend to support the new templates.
Composition.Programmer 2: Implement all missing HTML template files in the Flask application's templates directory as per the detailed page design requirements in the original task. This includes creating 'dashboard.html' with the specified elements and IDs, and all other templates referenced in main.py (e.g., membership_plans.html, plan_details.html, class_schedule.html, trainer_profiles.html, trainer_detail.html, pt_booking.html, pt_booking_confirmation.html, workout_records.html, log_workout.html). Each template must strictly follow the element IDs and page titles defined in the requirements document to ensure frontend-backend integration.

Composition.Programmer 1:
Test whether the website can be accessed through local port 5000. In this test, you only need to test whether the first page of the website can be accessed through the local 5000 port.
Task 2  Test whether the first page of the website loads correctly and whether basic navigation works based on the example data provided in the Task.
Task 3  Test the elements and integrity of all pages. This includes verifying the presence and correctness of all specified elements on each page as per the requirements

background_prompt: EvoMAC is a software company powered by multiple intelligent agents, such as chief executive officer, chief human resources officer, chief product officer, chief technology officer, etc, with a multi-agent organizational structure and the mission of 'changing the digital world through programming'.
